rulings.Case Title: BRIAN ROBERTS et al VS. ALFA LAVAL INC SUED INDIVIDUALLLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 22, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Roberts was exposed to any asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant, including Cutler-Hammer products. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiffs¿ second supplemental interrogatory responses are overruled. The court considered these responses. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Roberts, Mr. Boness and Mr. Germain are overruled. The court did not consider the evidence submitted with defendant¿s reply papers. = (220/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. PALOMARES VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 22, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 3. Plaintiff¿s motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted based on the liberal policy of allowing amendments. Defendants have shown that some prejudice against them will result by granting the motion. Therefore, the current trial date is vacated and continued to May 9 at 11:15am. All deadlines for discovery and summary motions now track the new trial date. = (220/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA PHILLIPS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 22, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 5. THIS IS A LATE TENTATIVE RULING. Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. It is overruled as to the issue whether Merle Phillips died from an asbestos-related illness; the Complaint states facts sufficient to allege he did. The Court does not take judicial notice of the death certificate. Even if the court were to take judicial notice of the existence of the death certificate as part of the records of the court (E.C. 452(d)), the Court does not take judicial notice of the truth of the facts stated in the death certificate itself for purposes of ruling on this demurrer. As to the second ground for the general demurrer, it is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath. = (220/MJM)
